author: Agent D
scope: BCP Dev v0.2 allocation / accounting tool family. BCPD-in-scope DevCos only: BCPD (BCP Development LLC), BCPBL (BCP Ben Lomond), ASD (Arrowhead Springs Development), BCPI (BCP Ironton). Hillcrest / Flagship Belmont are explicitly out of scope.
purpose: Hand-authored, value-by-value crosswalks between source-system vocabularies (ClickUp, QBD, Allocation Workbook, JCSList, Vertical Financials, DataRails 38-col, ClickUp lifecycle) and the canonical (community, phase, lot, account, prefix, cost_pool) tuples used by v0.2 tools. Crosswalks are first-class operating state; do not 'fix' source names — map them.

confidence_vocabulary.high: Identity mapping or unambiguous resolution corroborated by multiple sources or a clear naming convention.
confidence_vocabulary.medium: Single typo or short abbreviation requiring small inference; or single-source corroboration.
confidence_vocabulary.low: Heuristic; single-source historical, or naming pattern that can collide.
confidence_vocabulary.inferred-unknown: Mapping could not be made; held in holding bucket pending source-owner validation.
confidence_vocabulary.pending_source_doc_review: Authored from briefing / observed data; awaiting .docx/.pptx original verification.
confidence_vocabulary.workbook_replicated: Computed by replicating the Flagship Allocation Workbook v3 formula on populated cells.

validation_status_vocabulary.source_doc_extracted: Pulled directly from a source file currently in data/raw/ or in repo root.
validation_status_vocabulary.inferred_from_briefing: Authored from stakeholder briefing; not yet validated against original .docx/.pptx.
validation_status_vocabulary.observed_in_data: Confirmed by direct inspection of an in-repo CSV row.
validation_status_vocabulary.source_owner_validated: Validated by the human source owner (e.g., Streamline / accounting).

row_schema.monitored_field: True if the source value is expected to change over time and a tool must alert on drift.
row_schema.update_cadence: Recommended re-check frequency: 'never' (immutable), 'on_new_phase' (when a new ClickUp Phase task is created), 'monthly' (with the monthly Inventory-by-Job review), 'on_workbook_refresh' (when allocation workbook is republished), 'quarterly', 'on_demand'.

tables.name: phase_identifier_crosswalk
tables.description: Phase identifier across systems: ClickUp `phase` field, QBD job `Phase` column (JCSList col D), Allocation Workbook `Phase` column, JCSList `QB Job` (the job-name string), and Vertical Financials project_code where the code encodes phase. The same logical phase is named differently in each system.

rows.evidence: JCSList row 'BCP Development 03 Active Harmony B1' (Phase=B1, QB Job=Harmony B1)
rows.notes: ClickUp phase field also 'B1' for this community; VF code 'Harm3' is the B1/B2 grain encoded.

rows.evidence: JCSList row Phase=B2 QB Job=Harmony B2
rows.notes: Distinct from MF1/MF2; lot-number collisions with MF1 are real. Use 3-tuple join.

rows.evidence: JCSList row 'BCP Development 03 Active Harmony A14' (Phase=Mapping needed)
rows.notes: Phase column blank in JCSList; QB Job carries identifier. ClickUp also tags 'H A14 1415 Dug' with phase encoded in name. Crosswalk required.

rows.evidence: JCSList row 'BCP Development 03 Active Harmony Nathan' (Project=Harmony, Phase=Mapping needed, QB Job=Nathan)
rows.notes: Sub-phase / parcel name. PLJ workbook shows it as a separate community column. Treat as Harmony::Nathan phase.

rows.evidence: JCSList row 'BCP Development 03 Active Harmony Waddell'
rows.notes: Sub-phase / parcel name. Treat as Harmony::Waddell.

rows.evidence: LH Allocation 2025.10.xlsx - PLJ.csv header 'Harmony Phase A Plat 4'
rows.notes: PLJ column header 'Harmony Phase A Plat 4' → canonical 'Harmony::A4'. Same pattern for Plat 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 13, 14.
ClickUp uses 'MF1'; PLJ uses 'Multi-Family 1'. Lot-number collisions with B1 are documented in v2.1 (3-tuple join required).
Amenity sub-job; not a sellable phase. Cost-pool only.

rows.notes: DRIFT: QBD calls it 'Salem Phase 2' but the canonical phase identifier in MDA / workbook is 'B'. Critical mapping for accounting events.

tables.name: lot_numbering_convention
tables.description: Lot-number normalization rules + project-specific encoding caveats. Not a value-by-value mapping (14k+ rows live in data/staged/staged_lot_crosswalk_v0). v0.2 records the **rules** the tools must apply.

rows.notes: Strip leading zeros from numeric prefix, preserve alpha suffix (e.g., '0127B' → '127B').
Strip trailing '.0' from float-coerced lot numbers.

rows.source_value: <4-digit lot value where project=AultF or PWFS2>
rows.notes: AultF lot 101–211 → phase B1; PWFS2 lot 273–323 → phase B2. v0 mis-routed 1,499 rows / $4.0M. Decoder ships in v2.1 build.
401 rows of inferred-unknown AultF SR-suffix lots in v2.1. Hold for source-owner.
Harmony VF lot '1034' does not correspond to inventory lot 1034 — likely encodes phase 1 lot 34. Currently 53.7% match rate.

rows.source_value: <lot 101 in MF1 vs lot 101 in B1>
rows.evidence: state/aliases.json (Harmony 3-tuple join); CLAUDE.md data integrity rules
rows.notes: Harmony lots numbers REPEAT across phases. Tools MUST use 3-tuple (project, phase, lot) — flat (project, lot) joins are wrong.

rows.evidence: JCSList rows without recognizable LND/MPL/IND/DIR/WTR/CPI prefix
rows.notes: Current JCSList QB Job names (e.g., 'Harmony B1', 'Salem Phase 2') do NOT carry visible prefixes — prefix is implied by the row's parent grouping ('BCP Development 03 Active …'). v0.2 must extract prefix from the parent group label, not from QB Job. Flagged for source-owner: do prefixes appear on actual QBD job IDs, or are they encoded in the parent class hierarchy?

tables.name: lifecycle_status_to_event
tables.description: ClickUp lifecycle status label → AccountingEvent trigger. Subset shown here; canonical store is state/process_rules/status_taxonomy_v1.json and clickup_gl_event_map_v1.json. Included in CW so Agent E tools can validate that crosswalks resolve before allowing event detection.

rows.notes: Status entry = closing event.
Hard-gated on MDA day lot count check.
Tracking event only; no GL entry.
Requires FMV at Transfer.
Requires Sale Price.
Operational (homebuilder side). No DevCo GL event. gl_relevance=operational.
Operational only.
Operational pause between development and vertical. Possibly related to 'New Star' phase-code series in scope brief — UNRESOLVED.

unresolved_mappings.details: Scope brief mentions 'New Star / Harm3 / MF / B1 / B2 naming'. 'Harm3', 'MF', 'B1', 'B2' are all resolved (see CW-09). 'New Star' has no observable instance in the current JCSList / workbook / VF rows. The closest signal is ClickUp status 'waiting for new starts', which is a vertical-construction status — not a phase code. Source-owner clarification needed: is 'New Star' a phase-code series in QBD/VF, a vertical-build start-ID series, or shorthand for a homebuilder pause status?

unresolved_mappings.topic: Lewis Estates phase / Pioneer Village / Dry Creek Lehi triple-naming
unresolved_mappings.details: Lewis Estates Phase 1 appears in QBD as 'Pioneer Village' (job parent) and 'Dry Creek Lehi (Johnson)' (QB Job). No GL data flows through to Lewis Estates in v2.1 (zero match rate). Streamline must confirm whether 'Pioneer Village' is a community alias, a holding phase, or a legacy parent. Canonical phase ID held at Lewis Estates::1_Pioneer_Village.

Observed QBD job names ('Harmony B1', 'Parkway Fields E-1', 'Salem Phase 2') do NOT carry the LND/MPL/IND/DIR/WTR/CPI prefix that the account/prefix matrix expects. Prefix appears to be encoded in the parent class hierarchy ('BCP Development 03 Active …'). Need source-owner confirmation: are prefixes on actual QBD job IDs (not visible in our CSV export) or are they class-based?
JCSList rows show Project=Tooele but the QB job names reference 'Skywalk'. Is Skywalk a sub-community of Tooele, or has Tooele been renamed Skywalk? Held until 2025Status confirms.

unresolved_mappings.topic: Parkway Fields H1 / H2 phase collapse
unresolved_mappings.details: JCSList shows 'Parkway H1 Entitlements' and 'Parkway H2 Entitlements' as distinct QB jobs but both carry Phase=H. Intentional collapse, or naming drift?
VF code 'Harm3' = Harmony Phase 3. But Phase 3 contains B1, B2, B3, MF1, MF2 sub-phases (per PLJ headers). Is Harm3 a rollup or a phase peer?

Warranty pool referenced in ontology but not visible in Flagship Allocation Workbook v3 sheets. Confirm whether it lives in a different workbook (e.g., the JCS BCPD.xlsm and JCS ASD.xlsm files called out in scope which are not yet in repo) or is computed downstream.

QBD names use 'Salem Phase 2'/'Salem Phase 3'; canonical phase IDs are 'B'/'C'. The mapping itself is clear, but the source of truth for which is canonical (number or letter) is unresolved. v0.2 picks letter to match MDA/plat documents, pending Streamline confirmation.

monitored_fields_summary.description: Fields whose drift should trigger a refresh / alert. v0.2 tools should query crosswalks before computing.
must_alert_on_new_value: ClickUp.phase (any value not in CW-05 or CW-09)
JCSList.QBJob (any new job name not in CW-05)
must_alert_on_value_change: QBD customer name for an existing community (CW-02)
JCSList QB Job name for an existing phase (CW-05)
GL account label for existing posting/alloc codes (CW-10)
low_priority_monitor: VF project codes (CW-06) — legacy join, alert only on quarterly cadence
